title: Reflections Of A Magistrand. On Returning To St. Andrews
Horace's Philosophy
content:
What the end the gods have destined unto thee and unto me,
    Ask not: 'tis forbidden knowledge.    Be content, Leuconoe.
    Let alone the for

title: Milton. With Apologies To Lord Tennyson
content:
O swallow-tailed purveyor of college sprees,
    O skilled to please the student fraternity,
         Most honoured publican of Scotland,

title: A May-Day Madrigal
content:
The sun shines fair on Tweedside, the river flowing bright,
    Your heart is full of pleasure, your eyes are full of light,
    Your cheeks are

title: Golden Dream
content:
Golden dream of summer morn,
         By a well-remembered stream
    In the land where I was born,
                Golden dream!

    Ripples

title: Thou Art Queen
content:
Thou art queen to every eye,
         When the fairest maids convene.
    Envy's self can not deny
         Thou art queen.

    In thy step t

title: A Criticism Of Critics
content:
How often have the critics, trained
         To look upon the sky
    Through telescopes securely chained,
         Forgot the naked eye.

title: A Street Corner
content:
Here, where the thoroughfares meet at an angle
         Of ninety degrees (this angle is right),
    You may hear the loafers that jest and wran

title: After Waterloo
content:
On the field of Waterloo we made Napoleon rue
         That ever out of Elba he decided for to come,
    For we finished him that day, and he ha

title: Tears
content:
Mourn that which will not come again,
         The joy, the strength of early years.
         Bow down thy head, and let thy tears
    Water th

title: The M.A. Degree. After Wordsworth
content:
It was a phantom of delight
    When first it gleamed upon my sight,
    A scholarly distinction, sent
    To be a student's ornament.
    The

title: The Golf-Ball And The Loan. After Longfellow
content:
I drove a golf-ball into the air,
    It fell to earth, I knew not where;
    For, so swiftly it flew, the sight
    Could not follow it in its

title: The Wasted Day
content:
Another day let slip!    Its hours have run,
         Its golden hours, with prodigal excess,
         All run to waste.    A day of life the le

title: A Presentiment
content:
It seems a little word to say--
         _Farewell_--but may it not, when said,
         Be like the kiss we give the dead,
    Before they pas

title: The Solitary
content:
I have been lonely all my days on earth,
         Living a life within my secret soul,
    With mine own springs of sorrow and of mirth,

title: The Haunted Chamber
content:
Life is a house where many chambers be,
         And all the doors will yield to him who tries,
         Save one, whereof men say, behind it li

title: Music For The Dying. From The French Of Sully Prudhomme
content:
Ye who will help me in my dying pain,
         Speak not a word: let all your voices cease.
    Let me but hear some soft harmonious strain,

title: Adventure Of A Poet
content:
As I was walking down the street
         A week ago,
    Near Henderson's I chanced to meet
         A man I know.

    His name is Alexander

title: The Garden Of Sin
content:
I know the garden-close of sin,
         The cloying fruits, the noxious flowers,
         I long have roamed the walks and bowers,
    Desirin

title: To The Reader Of 'University Notes'
content:
Ah yes, we know what you're saying,
         As your eye glances over these Notes:
    'What asses are these that are braying
         With fla

title: The House Of Sleep
content:
When we have laid aside our last endeavour,
         And said farewell to one or two that weep,
    And issued from the house of life for ever,

title: The Crown Of Years
content:
Years grow and gather--each a gem
         Lustrous with laughter and with tears,
         And cunning Time a crown of years
    Contrives for

title: A Lover's Confession
content:
When people tell me they have loved
         But once in youth,
    I wonder, are they always moved
         To speak the truth?

    Not that

title: Ursula
content:
There is a village in a southern land,
    By rounded hills closed in on every hand.
    The streets slope steeply to the market-square,
    Lo

title: Winter At St. Andrews
content:
The city once again doth wear
         Her wonted dress of winter's bride,
    Her mantle woven of misty air,
         With saffron sunlight fa

title: One Tear
content:
Last night, when at parting
         Awhile we did stand,
    Suddenly starting,
         There fell on my hand

    Something that burned it,

title: To C. C. C.
content:
Oh for the nights when we used to sit
         In the firelight's glow or flicker,
    With the gas turned low and our pipes all lit,

title: Indolence
content:
Fain would I shake thee off, but weak am I
         Thy strong solicitations to withstand.
         Plenty of work lies ready to my hand,
    W

title: The True Liberal
content:
The truest Liberal is he
    Who sees the man in each degree,
    Who merit in a churl can prize,
    And baseness in an earl despise,
    Yet

title: For Scotland
content:
Beyond the Cheviots and the Tweed,
         Beyond the Firth of Forth,
    My memory returns at speed
         To Scotland and the North.

title: Art's Discipline
content:
Long since I came into the school of Art,
    A child in works, but not a child in heart.
    Slowly I learn, by her instruction mild,
    To b

title: The Outcast's Farewell
content:
The sun is banished,
    The daylight vanished,
    No rosy traces
         Are left behind.
    Here in the meadow
    I watch the shadow

title: Sleep Flies Me
content:
Sleep flies me like a lover
         Too eagerly pursued,
    Or like a bird to cover
         Within some distant wood,
    Where thickest bo

title: An Afterthought
content:
You found my life, a poor lame bird
         That had no heart to sing,
    You would not speak the magic word
         To give it voice and wi

title: Stanzas For Music
content:
I loved a little maiden
         In the golden years gone by;
    She lived in a mill, as they all do
         (There is doubtless a reason why

title: Midnight
content:
The air is dark and fragrant
         With memories of a shower,
    And sanctified with stillness
         By this most holy hour.

    The l

title: Undesired Revenge
content:
Sorrow and sin have worked their will
         For years upon your sovereign face,
         And yet it keeps a faded trace
    Of its unequalle

title: The First Meeting
content:
Last night for the first time, O Heart's Delight,
         I held your hand a moment in my own,
         The dearest moment which my soul has kn

title: Imitated From Wordsworth
content:
He brought a team from Inversnaid
         To play our Third Fifteen,
    A man whom none of us had played
         And very few had seen.

title: Sorrow's Treachery
content:
I made a truce last night with Sorrow,
         The queen of tears, the foe of sleep,
    To keep her tents until the morrow,
         Nor send

title: An Interview
content:
I met him down upon the pier;
         His eyes were wild and sad,
    And something in them made me fear
         That he was going mad.

title: On An Edinburgh Advocate
content:
In youth with diligence he toiled
         A Roman nose to gain,
    But though a decent pug was spoiled,
         A pug it did remain.

title: Welcome Home
content:
The fire burns bright
    And the hearth is clean swept,
    As she likes it kept,
    And the lamp is alight.
    She is coming to-night.

title: In Time Of Sickness
content:
Lost Youth, come back again!
    Laugh at weariness and pain.
    Come not in dreams, but come in truth,
                Lost Youth.

    Swee

title: Trafalgar Square
content:
These verses have I pilfered like a bee
    Out of a letter from my C. C. C.
         In London, showing what befell him there,
    With other

title: A Bunch Of Triolets
content:
TO ---

    You like the trifling triolet:
         Well, here are three or four.
    Unless your likings I forget,
    You like the trifling

title: The Life Of Earth
content:
The life of earth, how full of pain,
         Which greets us on our day of birth,
    Nor leaves us while we yet retain
                The li

title: A Swinburnian Interlude
content:
Short space shall be hereafter
         Ere April brings the hour
    Of weeping and of laughter,
         Of sunshine and of shower,
    Of g

title: Song Is Not Dead
content:
Song is not dead, although to-day
         Men tell us everything is said.
    There yet is something left to say,
         Song is not dead.

title: An Orator's Complaint
content:
How many the troubles that wait
         On mortals!--especially those
         Who endeavour in eloquent prose
    To expound their views, and

title: Moonlight North And South
content:
Love, we have heard together
         The North Sea sing his tune,
    And felt the wind's wild feather
         Brush past our cheeks at noon,

title: The Waster Singing At Midnight. After Longfellow.
content:
Loud he sang the song Ta Phershon
         For his personal diversion,
         Sang the chorus U-pi-dee,
         Sang about the Barley B

title: Lost At Sea
content:
Lost at sea, with all on board!
    No one saw their sinking sail,
    No one heard their dying wail,
    Heard them calling on the Lord--

title: Vivien's Song. At The L.L.A. Examination
content:
In Algebra, if Algebra be ours,
    x and x^2 can ne'er be equal powers,
    Unless x=1, or none at all.

    It is the little error in the sum

title: Come Back To St. Andrews
content:
Come back to St. Andrews!    Before you went away
    You said you would be wretched where you could not see the Bay,
    The East sands and the

title: My Lady
content:
My Lady of all ladies!    Queen by right
         Of tender beauty; full of gentle moods;
         With eyes that look divine beatitudes,
    L

title: Patriotism
content:
There was a time when it was counted high
         To be a patriot--whether by the zeal
         Of peaceful labour for the country's weal,

title: A Ballad Of Refreshment
content:
The lady stood at the station bar,
         (Three currants in a bun)
    And oh she was proud, as ladies are.
         (And the bun was baked

title: An Exile's Song
content:
My soul is like a prisoned lark,
         That sings and dreams of liberty,
    The nights are long, the days are dark,
         Away from home

title: Death At The Window
content:
This morning, while we sat in talk
         Of spring and apple-bloom,
    Lo!    Death stood in the garden walk,
         And peered into the

title: The Banished Bejant. From The Unpublished Remains Of Edgar Allan Poe
content:
In the oldest of our alleys,
         By good bejants tenanted,
    Once a man whose name was Wallace--
         William Wallace--reared his he

title: Poets
content:
Children of earth are we,
    Lovers of land and sea,
    Of hill, of brook, of tree,
         Of all things fair;
    Of all things dark or b

title: A Song Of Truce
content:
Till the tread of marching feet
    Through the quiet grass-grown street
    Of the little town shall come,
    Soldier, rest awhile at home.

title: A Lost Opportunity
content:
One dark, dark night--it was long ago,
         The air was heavy and still and warm--
    It fell to me and a man I know,
         To see two

title: Thirty Years After
content: Two old St. Andrews men, after a separation of nearly thirty years, meet by chance at a wayside inn.    They interchange experiences; and at lengt

title: Fickle Summer
content:
Fickle Summer's fled away,
         Shall we see her face again?
         Hearken to the weeping rain,
    Never sunbeam greets the day.

title: The End Of April
content:
This is the time when larks are singing loud
         And higher still ascending and more high,
    This is the time when many a fleecy cloud

title: Footsteps In The Street
content:
Oh, will the footsteps never be done?
                The insolent feet
                Thronging the street,
    Forsaken now of the only one.

title: The Death Of William Rufus
content:
The Red King's gone a-hunting, in the woods his father made
    For the tall red deer to wander through the thicket and the glade,
    The King

title: In Time Of Doubt
content:
'In the shadow of Thy wings, O Lord of Hosts, whom I extol,
    I will put my trust for ever,' so the kingly David sings.
    'Thou shalt help m

title: Pleasant Prophecies
content:
A day of gladness yet will dawn,
         Though when I cannot say;
    Perhaps it may be Thursday week,
         Perhaps some other day,--

title: Below Her Window
content:
Where she sleeps, no moonlight shines
         No pale beam unbidden creeps.
    Darkest shade the place enshrines
                Where she sl

title: Lost Liberty
content:
Of our own will we are not free,
         When freedom lies within our power.
         We wait for some decisive hour,
    To rise and take our

title: The City Of Golf
content:
Would you like to see a city given over,
         Soul and body, to a tyrannising game?
    If you would, there's little need to be a rover,

title: A Late Good Night
content:
My lamp is out, my task is done,
         And up the stair with lingering feet
    I climb.    The staircase clock strikes one.
         Good n

title: Vanity Of Vanities
content:
Be ye happy, if ye may,
    In the years that pass away.
    Ye shall pass and be forgot,
    And your place shall know you not.

    Other ge

title: The Close Of The Session
content:
The Session's over.    We must say farewell
         To these east winds and to this eastern sea,
         For summer comes, with swallow and wi

title: A Song Of Greek Prose
content:
Thrice happy are those
         Who ne'er heard of Greek Prose--
    Or Greek Poetry either, as far as that goes;
         For Liddell and

title: A Summer Morning
content:
Never was sun so bright before,
         No matin of the lark so sweet,
         No grass so green beneath my feet,
    Nor with such dewdrops

title: Make-Believes
content:
When I was young and well and glad,
    I used to play at being sad;
    Now youth and health are fled away,
    At being glad I sometimes play

title: The Voice That Sings
content:
The voice that sings across the night
         Of long forgotten days and things,
    Is there an ear to hear aright
         The voice that si

content:
As those who hear a sweet bird sing,
         And love each song it sings the best,
    Grieve when they see it taking wing
         And flying

title: To Number 27X.
content:
Beloved Peeler! friend and guide
                And guard of many a midnight reeler,
         None worthier, though the world is wide,

title: Greek Title
content:
I love the inoffensive frog,
         'A little child, a limber elf,'
    With health and spirits all agog,
    He does the long jump in a bog

title: The Swallows. From Jean Pierre Claris Florian
content:
I love to see the swallows come
         At my window twittering,
    Bringing from their southern home
         News of the approaching spring

title: To J. R.
content:
Last Sunday night I read the saddening story
         Of the unanswered love of fair Elaine,
    The 'faith unfaithful' and the joyless glory

title: Hymn Of Hippolytus To Artemis
content:
Artemis! thou fairest
    Of the maids that be
    In divine Olympus,
    Hail!    Hail to thee!
    To thee I bring this woven weed
    Cull

title: The Waster's Presentiment
The Fiddler
content:
There's a fiddler in the street,
         And the children all are dancing:
    Two dozen lightsome feet
         Springing and prancing.

title: Love Recalled In Sleep
content:
There was a time when in your face
         There dwelt such power, and in your smile
    I know not what of magic grace;
         They held me

title: The Caged Thrush
Dawn Song
content:
I hear a twittering of birds,
         And now they burst in song.
    How sweet, although it wants the words!
         It shall not want them

title: Requiem
content:
For thee the birds shall never sing again,
         Nor fresh green leaves come out upon the tree,
    The brook shall no more murmur the refrai

title: After Many Days
content:
The mist hangs round the College tower,
         The ghostly street
    Is silent at this midnight hour,
         Save for my feet.

    With

title: A College Career
content:
I

    When one is young and eager,
         A bejant and a boy,
    Though his moustache be meagre,
         That cannot mar his joy

title: Love's Phantom
content:
Whene'er I try to read a book,
    Across the page your face will look,
    And then I neither know nor care
    What sense the printed words m

title: On A Crushed Hat
content:
Brown was my friend, and faithful--but so fat!
         He came to see me in the twilight dim;
         I rose politely and invited him
    To

title: To Alfred Tennyson--1883
content:
Familiar with thy melody,
         We go debating of its power,
         As churls, who hear it hour by hour,
    Contemn the skylark's minstre

title: In Time Of Sorrow
content:
Despair is in the suns that shine,
         And in the rains that fall,
    This sad forsaken soul of mine
         Is weary of them all.

title: For A Present Of Roses
content:
Crimson and cream and white--
         My room is a garden of roses!
    Centre and left and right,
         Three several splendid posies.

title: Catullus At His Brother's Grave
content:
Through many lands and over many seas
    I come, my Brother, to thine obsequies,
    To pay thee the last honours that remain,
    And call up

title: The Delights Of Mathematics
content:
It seems a hundred years or more
         Since I, with note-book, ink and pen,
    In cap and gown, first trod the floor
         Which I have

title: A New Song To An Old Tune--From Victor Hugo
content:
If a pleasant lawn there grow
         By the showers caressed,
    Where in all the seasons blow
         Flowers gaily dressed,
    Where by

title: An Invitation
content:
Dear Ritchie, I am waiting for the signal word to fly,
         And tell me that the visit which has suffered such belating
    Is to be a thing

title: Love's Worship Restored
content:
O Love, thine empire is not dead,
    Nor will we let thy worship go,
    Although thine early flush be fled,
    Thine ardent eyes more faintl

title: Cyclamen
content:
I had a plant which would not thrive,
         Although I watered it with care,
         I could not save the blossoms fair,
    Nor even keep

title: A Birthday Gift
content:
No gift I bring but worship, and the love
         Which all must bear to lovely souls and pure,
         Those lights, that, when all else is d

title: A Christmas Fancy
content:
Early on Christmas Day,
                Love, as awake I lay,
    And heard the Christmas bells ring sweet and clearly,

title: A Ballad Of The Town Water
content:
It is the Police Commissioners,
         All on a winter's day;
    And they to prove the town water
         Have set themselves away.

    T

title: Youth Renewed
content:
When one who has wandered out of the way
         Which leads to the hills of joy,
    Whose heart has grown both cold and grey,
         Thoug

title: Sweetheart
content:
Sweetheart, that thou art fair I know,
         More fair to me
    Than flowers that make the loveliest show
         To tempt the bee.

title: Ichabod
content:
Gone is the glory from the hills,
         The autumn sunshine from the mere,
         Which mourns for the declining year
    In all her tribu

title: The Tempted Soul
content:
Weak soul, by sense still led astray,
         Why wilt thou parley with the foe?
         He seeks to work thine overthrow,
    And thou, po

title: Magni Nominis Umbra
content:
St. Andrews! not for ever thine shall be
         Merely the shadow of a mighty name,
         The remnant only of an ancient fame
    Which ti

title: The Poet's Hat
content:
The rain had fallen, the Poet arose,
         He passed through the doorway into the street,
    A strong wind lifted his hat from his head,

title: Yet A Little Sleep
content:
Beside the drowsy streams that creep
         Within this island of repose,
         Oh, let us rest from cares and woes,
    Oh, let us fold o

title: The Science Club
content:
Hurrah for the Science Club!
         Join it, ye fourth year men;
    Join it, thou smooth-cheeked scrub,
         Whose years scarce number t

title: Cairnsmill Den--Tune: 'A Roving'
content:
As I, with hopeless love o'erthrown,
    With love o'erthrown, with love o'erthrown,
         And this is truth I tell,
    As I, with hopeless

title: Nightfall
content:
Let me sleep.    The day is past,
         And the folded shadows keep
    Weary mortals safe and fast.
                Let me sleep.

    I a

title: Triolet
Where's The Use
content:
Oh, where's the use of having gifts that can't be turned to money?
         And where's the use of singing, when there's no one wants to hear?

title: A Coincidence
content:
Every critic in the town
    Runs the minor poet down;
    Every critic--don't you know it?
    Is himself a minor poet.

title: At A High Ceremony
content:
Not the proudest damsel here
    Looks so well as doth my dear.
    All the borrowed light of dress
    Outshining not her loveliness,

    A

title: A December Day
content:
Blue, blue is the sea to-day,
                Warmly the light
         Sleeps on St. Andrews Bay--
                Blue, fringed with whi

title: The Burial Of William The Conqueror
content:
Oh, who may this dead warrior be
         That to his grave they bring?
    'Tis William, Duke of Normandy,
         The conqueror and king.

title: The Best Pipe
content:
In vain you fervently extol,
         In vain you puff, your cutty clay.
    A twelvemonth smoked and black as coal,
         'Tis redolent of

title: Andrew M'Crie. From The Unpublished Remains Of Edgar Allan Poe
content:
It was many and many a year ago,
         In a city by the sea,
    That a man there lived whom I happened to know
         By the name of Andr

title: Hope Deferred
content:
When the weary night is fled,
    And the morning sky is red,
    Then my heart doth rise and say,
    'Surely she will come to-day.'

    In

title: Song From 'The Princess'
content:
As through the street at eve we went
         (It might be half-past ten),
    We fell out, my friend and I,
    About the cube of x+y,

title: Partnership In Fame
content:
Love, when the present is become the past,
         And dust has covered all that now is new,
         When many a fame has faded out of view,
